task_completion.description: The 'Sample Order Lines' dataset is imported and visible in the Oracle Analytics Desktop data panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'sample_order_lines2023' or 'Sample Order Lines' listed in the Data Elements pane on the left side of the Oracle Analytics Desktop interface.

task_completion.description: A bar chart visualization is created on the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A bar chart is visible in the main canvas area, and the visualization type icon indicates a Bar Chart.

task_completion.description: The 'Quantity Ordered' column is used on the X-axis and treated as a discrete attribute.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the grammar panel (Category/X-axis). 'Quantity Ordered' should be placed there. The X-axis of the chart must show discrete numbers (e.g., 1, 2, 3...) rather than a continuous aggregated measure.

task_completion.description: The Y-axis displays the count or frequency of orders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the grammar panel (Values/Y-axis). It should contain a count metric, such as 'Count of Quantity Ordered' or 'Number of Rows'. The Y-axis labels should reflect these frequency counts.

task_completion.description: The bar chart is sorted by the discrete quantity sizes in ascending order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The X-axis values (Quantity Ordered) must be ordered sequentially from lowest to highest (e.g., 1, 2, 3...) from left to right.

task_completion.description: The chart title is set to 'Line Item Quantity Frequency'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Line Item Quantity Frequency' is clearly visible as the title of the bar chart visualization.

task_completion.description: The workbook is saved with the name 'quantity_distribution'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The top title bar of the Oracle Analytics Desktop window or the save dialog shows the name 'quantity_distribution'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Oracle Analytics Desktop GUI to create the chart.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual Oracle Analytics Desktop application interface being interacted with, rather than a terminal running Python/R scripts to generate a chart.

integrity.description: The chart reflects genuine data from the dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: The bar chart shows realistic varying heights and X-axis values (up to ~170), indicating it is rendering the actual dataset and not a mock drawing or manually typed placeholder.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply create an empty file with the expected name.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is visual proof of the chart being constructed and saved within the Oracle Analytics application, rather than just a file explorer showing a renamed empty file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected filename is 'quantity_distribution.dva'. The expected chart title is 'Line Item Quantity Frequency'. The dataset is located at 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\OracleAnalyticsData\sample_order_lines2023.xlsx'. Crucially, the dataset column for quantity is named 'Quantity Ordered'. For the chart to be correct, 'Quantity Ordered' must be used on the X-axis and its treatment changed from a Measure to an Attribute so discrete numbers (1, 2, 3...) appear. The Y-axis must show the count/frequency of these orders. The X-axis must be sorted in ascending order.
